recommendation: Prioritize. This is a direct match to the candidate's digital-health, membership-growth, product-organization, and enterprise-channel experience. Confirm that the role has company-level investment authority rather than only ownership of a member-experience feature area.
role_brief: The role owns the member journey across onboarding, engagement, outcomes, and retention, while managing product managers and influencing company portfolio decisions.
company_brief: Northstar Health is fictional demonstration data representing a scaled virtual-care company serving members through health plans and employers.
evidence_match: Lead with Hearthline Health membership growth, activation improvement, five national health plans, and product-organization building. Add Atlas Cloud team leadership and operating mechanisms.
gaps_risks: The candidate should clarify clinical-accountability boundaries, Board exposure, team size, and whether the role controls growth and care-delivery experiences.
diligence_questions: Which member outcomes does Product own? What investment decisions can this leader make? How are Product and Clinical responsibilities divided? What must improve in the first year?
interview_packet: Prepare stories on building Hearthline's team, improving activation, scaling membership, enterprise health-plan work, and Atlas Cloud portfolio leadership.
outreach: I have built and scaled digital-health member experiences and product organizations across virtual care and enterprise AI. The combination of member outcomes, product leadership, and company-level strategy is closely aligned with the work I want to do next.
cover_letter: The fictional candidate's experience at Hearthline Health and Atlas Cloud directly supports Northstar Health's need for a leader who can connect member insight, clinical outcomes, platform strategy, and scaled adoption.
resume: Prioritize Hearthline Health, Atlas Cloud team leadership, membership growth, activation, health-plan work, and outcomes measurement.
